title: astragalus claranus
intro: Astragalus claranus, also known as milk vetch, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant is valued for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing stress, improving immune function, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Astragalus claranus can be prepared as a herbal tea, supplement, or dried root, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
